name: Total Cholesterol
description: Total amount of cholesterol in the blood.

name: LDL Cholesterol
description: Low-density lipoprotein cholesterol.

name: HDL Cholesterol
description: High-density lipoprotein cholesterol.

name: Triglycerides
description: Blood fat level.

name: Glucose
description: Fasting blood sugar level.

name: HbA1c
description: Average blood glucose over 2-3 months.

name: TSH
description: Thyroid-stimulating hormone.

name: Free T4
description: Free thyroxine hormone.

name: Vitamin D
description: 25-hydroxyvitamin D level.

name: Vitamin B12
description: Vitamin B12 level.

name: Ferritin
description: Iron storage protein.

name: WBC
description: White blood cell count.

name: Hemoglobin
description: Oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells.

name: Creatinine
description: Kidney function marker.

name: eGFR
description: Estimated glomerular filtration rate (kidney function).

name: ALT
description: Liver enzyme; elevated levels suggest liver stress.

name: AST
description: Liver and heart enzyme.

name: CRP
description: Inflammatory marker.

name: Homocysteine
description: Amino acid; elevated levels linked to cardiovascular risk.

name: Haematocrit
description: Proportion of blood volume occupied by red blood cells.

name: RBC
description: Red blood cell count.

name: MCV
description: Average volume of red blood cells.

name: MCH
description: Average amount of haemoglobin per red blood cell.

name: MCHC
description: Average concentration of haemoglobin in red blood cells.

name: RDW
description: Variation in red blood cell size.

name: Platelets
description: Platelet count.

name: MPV
description: Average size of platelets.

name: Neutrophils
description: Neutrophil white blood cell count.

name: Lymphocytes
description: Lymphocyte white blood cell count.

name: Monocytes
description: Monocyte white blood cell count.

name: Eosinophils
description: Eosinophil white blood cell count.

name: Basophils
description: Basophil white blood cell count.

name: ESR
description: Erythrocyte sedimentation rate, a marker of inflammation.

name: Total Bilirubin
description: Total bilirubin, a liver function marker.

name: ALP
description: Alkaline phosphatase liver enzyme.

name: GGT
description: Gamma-glutamyl transferase liver enzyme.

name: Total Protein
description: Total serum protein.

name: Albumin
description: Albumin protein level, marker of liver function and nutrition.

name: Globulins
description: Calculated as Total Protein minus Albumin.

name: Urea
description: Blood urea, a kidney function marker.

name: Calcium
description: Total serum calcium.

name: Adjusted Calcium
description: Calcium corrected for albumin level.

name: Phosphate
description: Serum phosphate.

name: Urate
description: Serum urate (uric acid), elevated in gout.

name: Sodium
description: Serum sodium electrolyte.

name: Potassium
description: Serum potassium electrolyte.

name: Chloride
description: Serum chloride electrolyte.

name: Bicarbonate
description: Serum bicarbonate, reflects acid-base balance.

name: Magnesium
description: Serum magnesium.

name: Iron
description: Serum iron level.

name: UIBC
description: Unsaturated iron-binding capacity.

name: TIBC
description: Total iron-binding capacity.

name: Transferrin
description: Transferrin protein that transports iron.

name: Transferrin Saturation
description: Percentage of transferrin saturated with iron.

name: Free T3
description: Free triiodothyronine (active thyroid hormone).

name: Cortisol
description: Cortisol (stress hormone), typically measured in morning.

name: Insulin
description: Fasting insulin level.

name: PSA
description: Prostate-specific antigen.

name: Folate
description: Serum folate (folic acid, vitamin B9).

name: Zinc
description: Serum zinc.

name: Copper
description: Serum copper.

name: Fibrinogen
description: Fibrinogen, a coagulation factor and inflammatory marker.

name: HsCRP
description: High-sensitivity C-reactive protein, cardiovascular risk marker.

name: Testosterone (Male)
description: Total testosterone in males. Optimal 600-900 ng/dL (functional medicine); clinical normal 270-1070 ng/dL.

name: Testosterone (Female)
description: Total testosterone in females. Optimal 40-60 ng/dL; clinical normal 15-70 ng/dL.

name: SHBG (Male)
description: Sex hormone-binding globulin in males. Optimal 20-30 nmol/L; clinical 10-57 nmol/L.

name: SHBG (Female)
description: Sex hormone-binding globulin in females. Optimal 40-80 nmol/L; clinical 18-144 nmol/L.

name: LH (Male)
description: Luteinising hormone in males. Optimal 3-10 IU/L; clinical 1.5-9.3 IU/L.

name: LH (Female)
description: Luteinising hormone in females (follicular phase reference). Optimal 2-15 IU/L; clinical 1.9-12.5 IU/L.

name: FSH (Male)
description: Follicle-stimulating hormone in males. Optimal 2-8 IU/L; clinical 1.5-12.4 IU/L.

name: FSH (Female)
description: Follicle-stimulating hormone in females (follicular phase reference). Optimal 3-10 IU/L; clinical 2.5-10.2 IU/L.

name: Estradiol (Male)
description: Estradiol (E2) in males. Optimal 73-110 pmol/L (20-30 pg/mL); clinical 25-155 pmol/L.

name: Estradiol (Female)
description: Estradiol (E2) in females (follicular phase reference). Optimal 294-734 pmol/L (80-200 pg/mL); clinical 110-1469 pmol/L.

name: Progesterone (Male)
description: Progesterone in males. Optimal 0.95-3.8 nmol/L (0.3-1.2 ng/mL); clinical 0.32-4.45 nmol/L.

name: Progesterone (Female)
description: Progesterone in females (follicular phase reference). Optimal 1.59-9.54 nmol/L (0.5-3.0 ng/mL); clinical 0.32-11.13 nmol/L.

name: Prolactin (Male)
description: Prolactin in males. Optimal 105-210 mIU/L (5-10 ng/mL); clinical 45-375 mIU/L.

name: Prolactin (Female)
description: Prolactin in females (non-pregnant). Optimal 105-420 mIU/L (5-20 ng/mL); clinical 64-636 mIU/L.

name: DHEA-S (Male)
description: DHEA-Sulphate in males (broad adult range). Optimal 5.4-10.9 umol/L (200-400 ug/dL); clinical 2.7-13.6 umol/L.

name: DHEA-S (Female)
description: DHEA-Sulphate in females (broad adult range). Optimal 4.1-8.1 umol/L (150-300 ug/dL); clinical 1.9-10.9 umol/L.

name: Reverse T3
description: Reverse T3 — inactive form of T3. Elevated levels can block thyroid receptor sites. Optimal <15 ng/dL; clinical 9-24 ng/dL.

name: Total T3
description: Total triiodothyronine. Optimal 1.7-2.2 nmol/L (111-143 ng/dL); clinical 1.2-2.7 nmol/L.

name: Total T4
description: Total thyroxine. Optimal 80-120 nmol/L (6.2-9.3 ug/dL); clinical 58-154 nmol/L.

name: Thyroid Peroxidase Ab
description: Thyroid peroxidase antibodies. Elevated indicates autoimmune thyroid disease (Hashimoto's). Optimal <10 IU/mL; negative <35 IU/mL.

name: Thyroglobulin Ab
description: Thyroglobulin antibodies. Elevated indicates autoimmune thyroid disease. Optimal <11 IU/mL; negative <40 IU/mL.

name: Selenium
description: Serum selenium. Optimal 1.2-1.8 umol/L (95-142 ug/L); clinical 0.8-2.0 umol/L.

name: Vitamin A
description: Serum retinol (Vitamin A). Optimal 1.7-2.8 umol/L; clinical 1.05-3.49 umol/L.

name: Vitamin E
description: Serum alpha-tocopherol (Vitamin E). Optimal 25-35 umol/L; clinical 12-42 umol/L.

name: Lp(a)
description: Lipoprotein(a) — independent cardiovascular risk marker. Lower is better. Optimal <30 nmol/L; intermediate risk 30-75 nmol/L; high risk >125 nmol/L.

name: ApoB
description: Apolipoprotein B — key marker of atherogenic particle number. Optimal <0.8 g/L; clinical <1.0 g/L.

name: ApoA1
description: Apolipoprotein A1 — main protein of HDL; higher is cardioprotective. Optimal 1.4-1.8 g/L; clinical >1.2 g/L.

name: Troponin I
description: High-sensitivity cardiac Troponin I. Elevated indicates myocardial injury. Optimal 0-14 ng/L; clinical upper limit ~52 ng/L.

name: Troponin T
description: High-sensitivity cardiac Troponin T. Optimal 0-9 ng/L; clinical upper limit 14 ng/L.

name: BNP
description: B-type natriuretic peptide — marker of cardiac stress and heart failure. Optimal <40 pg/mL; clinical normal <100 pg/mL.

name: NT-proBNP
aliases: n terminal pro b-type natriuretic peptide
description: N-terminal pro-BNP — marker of cardiac stress. Optimal <75 pg/mL; clinical normal <125 pg/mL (age-dependent).

name: IL-6
description: Interleukin-6 — pro-inflammatory cytokine. Elevated in infection, inflammation, autoimmune disease. Optimal <2 pg/mL; clinical <7 pg/mL.

name: Direct Bilirubin
description: Conjugated (direct) bilirubin. Elevated indicates hepatic obstruction or liver disease. Optimal 0-5 umol/L; clinical 0-7 umol/L.

name: LDH
description: Lactate dehydrogenase — tissue damage marker. Optimal 140-220 U/L; clinical 140-280 U/L.

name: Cystatin C
description: Cystatin C — kidney function marker, less affected by muscle mass than creatinine. Optimal 0.51-0.80 mg/L; clinical 0.51-0.98 mg/L.

name: Microalbumin
description: Urine microalbumin — early marker of kidney damage. Optimal <15 mg/L; clinical normal <30 mg/L (microalbuminuria threshold).

name: UACR
description: Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio — sensitive marker for diabetic nephropathy. Optimal <10 mg/g; clinical normal <30 mg/g.

name: Lead (Blood)
description: Blood lead level. No safe level — lower is better. Optimal <0.10 umol/L (<2 ug/dL); action level >0.24 umol/L (>5 ug/dL).

name: Mercury (Blood)
description: Blood mercury level. Optimal <15 nmol/L (<3 ug/L); clinical normal <45 nmol/L (<9 ug/L).

name: Arsenic (Urine)
description: Urine total arsenic. Optimal <0.13 umol/L (<10 ug/L); clinical normal <0.47 umol/L (<35 ug/L).

name: Calprotectin
description: Fecal calprotectin — marker of intestinal inflammation. Elevated in IBD. Optimal <30 ug/g; clinical normal <50 ug/g.
